title: Ex-Celtic Marcus Smart recalls 2018 incident that almost made him lose his right hand
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: In 2018, Marcus Smart severed glass in his hand after punching a picture frame post-loss, requiring 20 stitches.
The injury came close to permanently disabling Smart's right hand and cost him 11 games that season.
Smart, now a Lakers guard, has since played for the Grizzlies and Wizards, overcoming multiple hand injuries.

title: Boston Marathon runner honors sister’s recovery from severe crash
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Michael Chiang is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of his sister's recovery after a severe car accident.
After the accident, Jennifer Chiang underwent surgery and rehabilitation, regaining her ability to walk and run a 5K.
Michael Chiang's participation aims to support Spaulding Rehabilitation, where he also completed his residency.

title: Body pulled from Wakefield lake believed to be that of missing man
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Police found a body in Lake Quannapowitt believed to be missing Wakefield man Jason Thomas, 45.
Jason Thomas vanished on December 12; his family raised funds for a private investigation into his disappearance.
Authorities await the medical examiner's confirmation of identity, but foul play is not suspected at this time.

title: Massachusetts online casino bill sent to study, effectively shelved for now
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts online casino gambling bill H. 4431 moved to study, effectively shelving it for 2026.
The bill proposed allowing digital slot machines and blackjack under Massachusetts Gaming Commission regulation.
Rep. David Muradian plans to refile the online casino bill for the 2027-2028 legislative session.

title: Police: Bus driver in Medford crash may have been asleep at the wheel
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: An MBTA bus driver allegedly crashed into cars and a home in Medford on March 8, possibly due to drowsiness.
Police stated that the driver, a 40-year-old man, faces charges including negligent operation and marked lanes violation.
No alcohol or intoxication is suspected, and weather conditions were clear at the time of the crash, according to police.

title: Black Mountain in N.H. will not become a community co-op as planned
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Black Mountain in Jackson, N.H., will not become a community co-op; it remains privately owned.
Erik Mogensen, who initially saved Black Mountain from closure, has purchased it for a second time.
Black Mountain will serve as a test site for addressing challenges faced by independent ski areas.

title: Why a 35-year-old breast cancer survivor is running the Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Julia Aldrich, 35, a breast cancer survivor from Cincinnati, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for charity.
Aldrich was diagnosed with breast cancer at 32 and resumed racing shortly after a double mastectomy.
She is fundraising for the Dana-Farber Cancer Institute, which played a role in her cancer care.

title: An expert explains why the recent Danvers murder stands out
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, 18, was arraigned for murder after allegedly stabbing Janet Swallows in Danvers as she slept.
James Alan Fox cites teenage impulsivity and underdeveloped brains as factors in seemingly senseless teen murders.
Experts note mental health and a lack of future thinking may contribute to impulsive acts of violence among teens.

title: MGB researchers just developed a new risk calculator for obesity. Here’s why that’s a game changer.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Mass General Brigham researchers developed a genetic risk calculator to predict obesity and Type 2 diabetes' long-term health impacts.
The tool uses data from over 8.5 million participants globally, enhancing its predictive accuracy across diverse populations.
The calculator anticipates intervention needs, offering potential for early prevention strategies against obesity-related complications.

title: Why this ICU nurse is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support trauma recovery
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Erin Daniel, a nurse, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for Brigham and Women's Hospital's Stepping Strong team.
Daniel is inspired by her ICU patients and the Stepping Strong Foundation's mission to advance trauma recovery.
Running the marathon honors Gillian Reny and supports research improving patient outcomes and promoting resilience.
